options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of items to return

list.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean droplets list` to list droplets. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`.

get.help: Get details for a specific droplet.
get.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean droplets get` to get details for a specific droplet. Required arguments: `ITEM_ID`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--properties/-p`.

search.help: Search droplets with wildcard pattern matching. Supports * wildcards for pattern matching across all string fields.
search.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean droplets search` to search droplets with wildcard pattern matching. Supports * wildcards for pattern matching across all string fields. Required arguments: `QUERY`. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--properties/-p`.

droplets.usage_instructions: Use `digitalocean droplets` for manage digitalocean droplets. Available subcommands here are `list`, `get`, `search`.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean auth login` to configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent. Common options: `--profile/-p`, `--force/-F`, `--credential-type/-c`.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean auth logout` to clear stored credentials. Common options: `--profile/-p`.

status.help: Check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean auth status` to check authentication status across profiles. Performs a live round-trip for every configured credential type so the report reflects ground truth — not on-disk belief. Saved credentials whose live verification fails are reported as ``authenticated: false`` with the failure reason in ``api_test``. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--profile/-p`.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean auth test` to test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--profile/-p`.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean auth profiles list` to list all profiles and show which is the default. Common options: `--table/-t`, `--limit/-l`, `--filter/-f`, `--properties/-p`.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean auth profiles get` to get details for a specific profile. Required arguments: `NAME`. Common options: `--table/-t`.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean auth profiles create` to create a new profile from .env.example template. Required arguments: `NAME`.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean auth profiles set-default` to set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1). Required arguments: `NAME`.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean auth profiles delete` to delete a profile and its data. Required arguments: `NAME`. Common options: `--force/-F`.

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `digitalocean auth profiles` for manage authentication profiles. Available subcommands here are `list`, `get`, `create`, `set-default`, `delete`.

auth.usage_instructions: Use `digitalocean auth` for manage digitalocean authentication. Available subcommands here are `login`, `logout`, `status`, `test`, `profiles`.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `digitalocean cache clear` to remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.

cache.usage_instructions: Use `digitalocean cache` for manage response cache. Available subcommands here are `clear`.

usage_instructions: Use for cli interface for digitalocean api. Commands follow `digitalocean <group> <action>`. Primary groups here are `digitalocean droplets`, `digitalocean auth`, `digitalocean cache`.
